channel.title: Sung Min Park, Tatsunori Hashimoto, Percy Liang, James Zou on
channel.description: Recent content in Sung Min Park, Tatsunori Hashimoto, Percy Liang, James Zou on

item.title: Data valuation for LLMs
item.description:
Introduction
#

One of the core components of building foundation models(LLMs) is designing the training data. A key challenge is estimating the value of a given data source. 1 Examples of questions include:

  • What are the values of different data sources 2 w.r.t to core model capabilities? This could be in an attempt to ablate existing data sources, or to decide which new ones to incorporate.
  • What was the counterfactual contribution of [some copyrighted work] on the model’s generations? How much should the authors be compensated?

The common underlying primitive needed to answer these questions is the ability to estimate the counterfactual effect of interventions on the training data on some downstream eval. While researchers routinely conduct coarse data ablations (e.g., we know that code contributes to general capability improvements CITE), anything that is more fine-grained quickly becomes intractable. For instance, estimating the marginal (“leave-one-out”) contribution of 100 data sources would—without further approximations—require re-training 100 times. As a result, one typically employs heuristics such as “micro-annealing,” but their fidelity remains unclear (CITE).
